books.rule_text:
Discussion is the goal

Do not post shallow content. All posts must be directly book related, informative, and discussion focused.

checklist.description: Is the post NOT directly related to books?
Is the post shallow, uninformative, or lacking a focus on meaningful discussion?

books.rule_text:
Personal conduct

Please use a civil tone and assume good faith when entering a conversation.

checklist.atmosphere_note: Threshold adjusted to 0.65 because the books community generally expects thoughtful, high-quality, and highly respectful literary discourse compared to more casual subreddits.
checklist.description: Does the content contain an uncivil tone, such as insults, personal attacks, condescension, or overt hostility towards another user?
Does the content fail to assume good faith by baselessly accusing other users of trolling, being shills, or acting with malicious intent?

examples.label: violating
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain an uncivil tone, such as insults, personal attacks, condescension, or overt hostility towards another user?
examples.relevance_note: Direct insult and hostile language directed at another user over a literary disagreement.

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain an uncivil tone, such as insults, personal attacks, condescension, or overt hostility towards another user?
examples.relevance_note: Passive-aggressive condescension regarding a user's reading comprehension; moderators may disagree if this crosses the line into a formal rule violation.
examples.label: borderline

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content fail to assume good faith by baselessly accusing other users of trolling, being shills, or acting with malicious intent?
examples.relevance_note: Explicitly calling another user a troll and accusing them of lying for attention.
examples.label: violating

examples.relevance_note: Accuses the user of deliberately misunderstanding or faking ignorance to win an argument; straddles the line between expressing frustration and assuming bad faith.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content fail to assume good faith by baselessly accusing other users of trolling, being shills, or acting with malicious intent?
examples.label: borderline

examples.relevance_note: Polite disagreement that addresses the argument itself without attacking the user or their motives.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content fail to assume good faith by baselessly accusing other users of trolling, being shills, or acting with malicious intent?
examples.label: compliant

books.rule_text:
Prohibited

Self-promotion and promotional posts, comments & flairs, memes, image-only, video-only, personal recommendation requests, 'what's that book' and 'should I read?' requests, sales links, piracy, plagiarism, low quality book lists, spoilers without warning, sensationalist headlines. Please see extended rules for appropriate subreddits or use the multireddit buttons.

checklist.rule_text_anchor: Self-promotion and promotional posts, comments & flairs
checklist.description: Does the content contain self-promotion, promotional material, or sales links?

checklist.rule_text_anchor: personal recommendation requests, 'what's that book' and 'should I read?' requests
checklist.description: Is the content a request for personal recommendations, help identifying a book, or asking whether to read a specific book?

checklist.atmosphere_note: Threshold lowered for image-only posts to enforce the community's strict preference for text-based discussion over visual 'book hauls' or bookshelf photos.
checklist.description: Is the content a meme, an image-only post, or a video-only post?

checklist.rule_text_anchor: piracy, plagiarism, low quality book lists, spoilers without warning, sensationalist headlines
checklist.description: Does the content contain piracy, plagiarism, unmarked spoilers, sensationalist headlines, or low-quality book lists?

examples.relevance_note: Direct self-promotion and sales link.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain self-promotion, promotional material, or sales links?
examples.label: violating

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain self-promotion, promotional material, or sales links?
examples.relevance_note: Mentions their own specific book by name but frames it as a discussion about libraries; moderators might debate if this is sneaky self-promotion.
examples.label: borderline

examples.relevance_note: Explicit 'what's that book' identification request.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a request for personal recommendations, help identifying a book, or asking whether to read a specific book?
examples.label: violating
borderline
examples.relevance_note: Technically asking if they should read it, but structured as a broader discussion about the book's complex formatting.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a request for personal recommendations, help identifying a book, or asking whether to read a specific book?

examples.relevance_note: Standard internet meme.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a meme, an image-only post, or a video-only post?
examples.label: violating

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a meme, an image-only post, or a video-only post?
examples.relevance_note: Image-only post, but the image contains a dense infographic with high-effort reviews. Might be borderline depending on strictness of 'image-only' enforcement.
examples.label: borderline

examples.relevance_note: Explicit request for pirated material.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain piracy, plagiarism, unmarked spoilers, sensationalist headlines, or low-quality book lists?
examples.label: violating
borderline

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain piracy, plagiarism, unmarked spoilers, sensationalist headlines, or low-quality book lists?

examples.label: compliant
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Does the content contain piracy, plagiarism, unmarked spoilers, sensationalist headlines, or low-quality book lists?

books.rule_text:
Encouraged

We love original content and self-posts! Thoughts, discussion questions, epiphanies and interesting links about authors and their work. We also encourage discussion about developments in the book world and we have a flair system.

checklist.rule_text_anchor: Thoughts, discussion questions, epiphanies and interesting links about authors and their work. We also encourage discussion about developments in the book world
checklist.description: Is the content entirely unrelated to books, authors, literature, or developments in the book world?

books.rule_text:
Important

We don't allow personal recommendation posts. You can ask in our Weekly Recommendation Thread, consult our Suggested Reading or What to Read page, or post in /r/suggestmeabook.

checklist.description: Is the content a standalone post asking for personal book recommendations?
checklist.rule_text_anchor: We don't allow personal recommendation posts.

examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a standalone post asking for personal book recommendations?
examples.label: violating
examples.relevance_note: Directly asks for personal recommendations based on personal taste, clearly violating the rule.

examples.label: borderline
examples.relevance_note: The user is asking for book suggestions, but framed within an academic/discussion context ('quintessential examples', 'writing a paper') rather than casual personal reading. Moderators might disagree on whether this constitutes a 'personal recommendation' post.
examples.related_checklist_item_description: Is the content a standalone post asking for personal book recommendations?

examples.label: compliant
examples.relevance_note: A standalone post meant to start a discussion about a specific book, with no request for recommendations.

books.rule_text:
Spoiler Policy

Any post with a spoiler in the title will be removed. Any comment with a spoiler that doesn't use the spoiler code will be removed. Any user with an extensive history of spoiling books will be banned. Spoiler tags are: #s for example: Spoilers about XYZ is done with: [Spoilers about XYZ](#s "Spoiler content here").

checklist.description: Is the item being evaluated a post (not a comment)?
checklist.rule_text_anchor: Any post with a spoiler in the title will be removed.

checklist.description: Is the item being evaluated a comment?

examples.relevance_note: Explicitly reveals a major character death in the post title.
examples.label: violating
borderline
examples.relevance_note: The title names a famous event ('Red Wedding') which hints at tragedy, but doesn't explicitly state what happens. Moderators might disagree on whether naming the event itself constitutes a spoiler.

examples.label: violating
examples.relevance_note: Contains a major plot twist fully exposed in plain text within a comment, ignoring the spoiler code.

The user successfully hid the spoiler, but used standard Reddit markdown (`>! !<`) instead of the specifically mandated subreddit spoiler code `[Warning](#s \"text\")`. This tests the strictness of the formatting rule.
examples.label: borderline
compliant
examples.relevance_note: Properly follows the exact spoiler code format required by the subreddit.
